selected_important_turn.text: I will need food packaging as well, so unfortunately I cannot agree on you taking it all. I will need 2 of the food packages, 1 of the firewood, and 2 of the water as I want to ensure to feed/hydrate my family of 5.. Does this work for you?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete, competitive allocation from YOU that rejects THEM's initial strong claim to all the food and sets a zero-sum tone. It shifts the dialogue from information-seeking to claim-making and frames the items as indivisible spoils rather than potential tradeables.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although it protects your needs, it is abrupt and claim-focused. It closes off integrative possibilities (e.g., exploring tradeoffs across items, eliciting the opponent's underlying reasons, or using proportional/fairness criteria). That makes escalation more likely and reduces chances for a mutually better deal.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the refusal and open integrative options. Instead of flatly rejecting, acknowledge their need, ask a brief question about underlying constraints (why they need all food/how flexible they are on water or firewood), and propose multiple options or a trade (e.g., a tentative split of food combined with compensating extra water/firewood for them or a fairness rule based on family size).

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by opening trade-offs and enabling creative packages)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (by inviting a fairness rule or proportional split)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (less defensive language and more options should lower resistance)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (shows empathy and willingness to collaborate rather than compete)

value2reason.Low: To stay hydrated on hot days
value2reason.Medium: To allow fire for cooking and keeping warm at night
value2reason.High: To ensure proper nutrition for 5 of us

value2reason.Low: Firewood will help me from cold and that I can have it as my last priority as other would carry that and I can seek help from them
value2reason.Medium: Water the most essential and basic need and that should be in sufficient amount while camping this will be needed.
value2reason.High: During the camping we need to take some good amount of food, as we can't predict when we get back and by what time we get food, so better to have good amount of food which is safe.

dialogue.text: Hi! how are you doing? I hope you are getting things packed for the camping.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hi there! I am doing well. I am getting things ready and so excited! How are you doing?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm great! and almost done with packing. and exited about the camping too. Waiting to explore and learn during the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That sounds great! What do you prefer with the additional packages for camping?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes I do! I was to ask you that in this conversation, will you be Okay with any agreement?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Let's see what we are both thinking and negotiate from there. What are your first thoughts on the additional camping packages?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need all the food packing and some basic needs for the camping, may I know about your first though on which you need additional package?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I will need food packaging as well, so unfortunately I cannot agree on you taking it all. I will need 2 of the food packages, 1 of the firewood, and 2 of the water as I want to ensure to feed/hydrate my family of 5.. Does this work for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand you need the food for the group, but I’m concerned about the balance. How flexible are you on splitting the food and adjusting water or firewood? Maybe we can find a fair split that works for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm willing to split the food evenly, but I'd like to ensure we both have enough water. How about 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood for you, and the rest for me? That way we both have sufficient food and water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That sounds like a good start. I'm happy to take 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. But just to clarify, are you comfortable with me taking 1 more firewood and you taking 1 less water? It would better balance our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with that adjustment. Let me confirm: you take 2 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood, and I take 1 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. That should balance our needs fairly.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That seems fair. Let me just confirm: I take 2 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood, and you take 1 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. We’ve split all 9 packages, right?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes, that’s correct. We’ve split all 9 packages. I’ll take 1 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood, and you take 2 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood. Let’s agree and then I’ll leave.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Great, I'm happy with this split. Let's confirm: I take 2 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood, and you take 1 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. All packages are accounted for, and I think this works well for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Agreed on the split. I’ll leave now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
